title: Smoky Kissed Middle-Eastern Lamb Shawarma
description: A crowd-pleasing Middle-Eastern dish featuring smoky paprika and fermented ingredients, low in carbs and perfect for a shawarma form on a cedar plank.

ingredients.name: Fermented Korean chili paste (gochujang)
ingredients.notes: Use sparingly, as it's quite spicy.

instructions: In a small bowl, mix together the smoked paprika, gochujang, garlic, lemon juice, salt, and black pepper.
Rub the spice mixture all over the lamb shoulder, making sure to get some under the skin as well.
Place the lamb on the grill and cook for 20 minutes per side, or until the internal temperature reaches 145°F (63°C).
Transfer the lamb to a cutting board and let it rest for 10 minutes before slicing it thinly against the grain.
Warm the flatbread on the grill for a few minutes on each side.
Assemble the shawarma by placing a few slices of lamb on each piece of flatbread, followed by a spoonful of tzatziki sauce, some pickled turnips, and a sprinkle of parsley.
Serve immediately, with cucumber-tomato salad on the side.
Garnish with extra parsley if desired.
Cut the cedar plank into 4-6 pieces for serving.
Serve each shawarma on a piece of cedar plank, with the salad and tzatziki sauce on the side.
Slice the lamb into thin strips and place on top of the salad.
Drizzle with a little extra lemon juice and olive oil if desired.
Garnish with parsley and serve immediately.
